message:
feat(playbook): add --repo flag for importing to workspace playbook

Enhances the playbook import command with the ability to target either
the global playbook (default) or the workspace/repo-specific playbook.

Changes:
- Add --repo flag to import subcommand flags interface
- When --repo is specified, resolve the git repository root and use
  <repo>/playbook.yaml as the target path instead of the global path
- Create the target directory if needed when importing to repo playbook
- Include target path in both JSON output and human-readable output
  for clarity on where rules were actually imported
- Error gracefully if --repo is used outside a git repository

Additional fix:
- Remove sourceAgents from exported bullets via prepareBulletForExport
- sourceAgents contains agent identity information that is both not
  portable between systems and potentially privacy-sensitive

Use case: Teams sharing playbooks can now import directly to the
workspace playbook that gets committed with their code, making rules
available to all team members without polluting global config.
